title: AC1-D-SPEC: accept durable dispatcher micro-spec
description:
## Goal and why
AC1-D-SPEC: accept durable dispatcher micro-spec: Produce and accept the micro-spec resolving dispatcher API, task/pi-session mapping, input correlation, durable task-state seam, audit actor/principal, stable errors, files, and proof matrix. Durable state is mandatory; exact T1 envelope vs narrow table remains an engineer choice.

## Scope
Implement one coherent, reviewable PR only; preserve Decision 21–24, workspace-first authority, and the plan's no-pure-mode rule.

## Explicit exclusions
No scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, control plane, generic policy framework, secret/raw-path output, or unrelated refactor. Do not silently widen this bead.

## Acceptance and proof
Add focused unit tests at the public seam and integration/e2e or host-proof coverage where this task crosses a runtime boundary. Run the package typecheck, targeted tests, and the exact plan acceptance gate; attach redacted command output/evidence to the PR.

## Risks / owner blockers
Hard block for AC1-D; no dispatcher code before acceptance.
acceptance_criteria: No code test is expected. Close only with an accepted committed AC1-D-SPEC that records all eight required decisions, its proof matrix, authoritative path, approver, and date.

title: D1-004d1 admission ledger core
description:
## Goal and why
D1-004d1 admission ledger core: add the durable Postgres admission ledger foundation — Drizzle migration/schema, admissionLedger.ts, session advisory fence, and CLI destructive-diff read — keyed by host, binding, and execution identity. Commit before effects, use advisory fencing, and fail closed on mismatch or commit failure. This is the reusable admission ledger core; it does not own consumer wiring or rollback journaling.

## Scope
One reviewable PR. ≤400 net lines. Preserve Decisions 21–24, workspace-first authority, and no pure mode. Scope is narrowed to the ledger core only: Drizzle migration/schema + admissionLedger.ts + session advisory fence + CLI destructive-diff read. D1-004d2 owns Pi first-effect admission consumers; D1-004d3 owns the WorkspaceBridge operation admission seam. D1-004e owns append-only rollback-journal creation, prepared-state reads, and recovery fencing. D1-005c is the first concrete stable-core consumer of the admission hook.

## Explicit exclusions
No rollback-journal reads or writes, publication pointer, served acknowledgement, scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, control plane, generic policy framework, secret/raw-path output, or unrelated refactor. No Pi service decorator, createAgent.ts facade, slash/reload hook wiring, or WorkspaceBridge operation wiring (those are D1-004d2/d3).

## Acceptance and proof
Focused unit tests prove admission rows and advisory fencing commit before the first effect and reject a mismatch/commit failure without effects. Run targeted typecheck/tests and attach redacted output.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance field: focused proof verifies only this bead's owned contract.

Acceptance Criteria:
Focused proof verifies: admitted binding remains non-removable after process restart and revision-directory cleanup; landing-only revisions preserve execution identity; binding-id reuse with different execution facts fails closed; concurrent admission is idempotent; kill-after-commit-before-effect leaves a deliberate permanent fence; and pre-commit/lock failure produces no effect. No rollback journal or stable-core publication behavior is implemented.

Owned stable codes: D1_ADMISSION_IDENTITY_MISMATCH, D1_ADMISSION_RECORD_FAILED (must match these names exactly — do not invent variants).

Split per sizing audit 2026-07-13; siblings D1-004d2/d3 carry the consumer seams (pre-declared in PR-PLAN).
acceptance_criteria:
Focused proof verifies: admitted binding remains non-removable after process restart and revision-directory cleanup; landing-only revisions preserve execution identity; binding-id reuse with different execution facts fails closed; concurrent admission is idempotent; kill-after-commit-before-effect leaves a deliberate permanent fence; and pre-commit/lock failure produces no effect. No rollback journal or stable-core publication behavior is implemented.

Owned stable codes: D1_ADMISSION_IDENTITY_MISMATCH, D1_ADMISSION_RECORD_FAILED (must match these names exactly — do not invent variants).

title: D1-004d3 WorkspaceBridge operation admission seam
description:
## Goal and why
D1-004d3 WorkspaceBridge operation admission seam: route direct WorkspaceBridge operations through the same admission check established by D1-004d1/D1-004d2, so no bridge operation can bypass the admission ledger.

## Scope
One reviewable PR. ≤220 net lines. Preserve Decisions 21–24, workspace-first authority, and no pure mode. Consumes the D1-004d1 admission ledger core and D1-004d2 consumer wiring pattern.

## Explicit exclusions
No Drizzle migration/schema changes, no rollback-journal reads/writes, no Pi service decorator/createAgent.ts/slash/reload hook changes (that is D1-004d2), no scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, control plane, or unrelated refactor.

## Acceptance and proof
Focused unit/integration tests prove a WorkspaceBridge operation without a committed admission row is refused, using D1-004d1's stable code (D1_ADMISSION_IDENTITY_MISMATCH — do not invent variants). Run targeted typecheck/tests and attach redacted output.

## Acceptance Criteria
Focused proof verifies a WorkspaceBridge operation without a committed admission row is refused, using D1-004d1's stable code (D1_ADMISSION_IDENTITY_MISMATCH — must match this name exactly — do not invent variants).

Split per sizing audit 2026-07-13; sibling of D1-004d1/D1-004d2 (pre-declared in PR-PLAN).

title: D1-005b observed host execution attestation
description:
## Goal and why
D1-005b observed host execution attestation: Inspect stopped migration/core/ingress artifacts against the approved release, enforce non-root/read-only/secret-safe migration and expand-only compatibility, then mint verified execution capability.

## Scope
Implement one coherent, reviewable PR only; preserve Decision 21–24, workspace-first authority, and the plan's no-pure-mode rule.

## Explicit exclusions
No scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, control plane, generic policy framework, secret/raw-path output, or unrelated refactor. Do not silently widen this bead.

## Acceptance and proof
Add focused unit tests at the public seam and integration/e2e or host-proof coverage where this task crosses a runtime boundary. Run the package typecheck, targeted tests, and the exact plan acceptance gate; attach redacted command output/evidence to the PR.

## Risks / owner blockers
No P6-R, admission, pointer mutation, or ingress start.

## Acceptance Criteria
Focused unit coverage plus the named integration, e2e, or EU-host proof passes. Stable-code, redaction, authority, and no-widening assertions must hold before merge.

SIZE FLAG (audit 2026-07-13): at the 400-line ceiling — if implementation overflows, STOP and pre-declare a 2-child stacked split before pushing.
acceptance_criteria:
Proof verifies a stopped one-shot migration container has DB-only access, non-root UID, read-only root, no state mounts, no privilege/capabilities, exact approved command/env, zero exit, and no secret canary in Docker inspect/config. An isolated-copy rehearsal proves both the new and immediately prior approved cores can boot and run read/write smoke after an expand-only migration; incompatible/contracting migrations reject before live DB change; observed drift quarantines the core with ingress stopped.

Owned stable codes: D1_ACTIVE_BINDING_RESTART_REQUIRED (must match these names exactly — do not invent variants).

title: D1-006 EU-host three-binding proof, DR rehearsal, and runbook
description:
## Goal and why
D1-006 EU-host three-binding proof, DR rehearsal, and runbook: Execute redacted EU-host proof for three workspaces/hostnames, N+1 continuity, rollback, isolation, secret canary, golden-path timing, and isolated backup/restore; write the bounded runbook.

## Scope
Implement one coherent, reviewable PR only; preserve Decision 21–24, workspace-first authority, and the plan's no-pure-mode rule.

## Explicit exclusions
No scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, control plane, generic policy framework, secret/raw-path output, or unrelated refactor. Do not silently widen this bead.

## Acceptance and proof
Add focused unit tests at the public seam and integration/e2e or host-proof coverage where this task crosses a runtime boundary. Run the package typecheck, targeted tests, and the exact plan acceptance gate; attach redacted command output/evidence to the PR.

## Risks / owner blockers
Production exit is blocked by accepted D1-006a evidence and the owner EU privilege/profile decision; 15 minutes is measured target, not a gate.

## Acceptance Criteria
Focused unit coverage plus the named integration, e2e, or EU-host proof passes. Stable-code, redaction, authority, and no-widening assertions must hold before merge.
acceptance_criteria: EU-host proof runs three independent P6-R bundles/workspaces/hostnames in one core revision; proves member/non-member/cross-binding denials before effects; N+1 continuity with retained identities byte-identical and no service restart; exact new-revision rollback removing only an unadmitted addition; stable-code failures for stale CAS, duplicates, root overlap, proxy confusion, unavailable secret, unsatisfied requirement, and partial readiness; D1-006a isolation suite plus secret canary; golden-path timing evidence; and isolated offline DR restore preserving admission/journal history, membership, digests, readable sessions, no ingress start, and recorded RPO/RTO. Requires accepted D1-006a evidence, D1-005c collection, and dated owner profile decision.

title: GH-579 T17d2 integrate shelf providers and contextual ownership
description:
## Goal
Integrate the presentational shelf with the workspace inventory/playback providers, above-editor mount, and existing contextual recording-surface registry without duplicating resources or capture chrome.

## Scope
- Mount T17d1 through the T17a0 above-editor seam.
- Subscribe to the workspace T15f/T17c RecordingInventoryStore and one workspace T17a PlaybackProvider; pass typed state/actions to presentational views.
- Keep the document subscription alive while empty UI returns null.
- Register the shelf as the sole contextual capture chrome through useWorkspaceRecordingFooterRegistration when its exact document owns the session.
- While shelf owns the origin document, suppress the existing DocumentRecordingAction status footer/elapsed/Stop and the capsule; companion remains transcript/open/delete only.
- On non-origin documents, shelf renders no capture controls and existing footer/capsule behavior remains unchanged.
- Render unavailable/disabled state emitted by playback; never stop/pause/revoke/create audio resources in UI effects.
- V1 adds no Delete button and no navigation implementation; pass disabled navigation contract until T17e.

## Non-goals
No presentational shelf redesign, responsive CSS, polling/client/status calls, audio/provider creation, Tiptap cutover, navigation wiring, delete action, or MarkdownEditor body edit.

## Acceptance
- [ ] One shelf/container subscription per mounted Markdown document and one workspace playback owner.
- [ ] Origin shelf is the sole contextual Stop/elapsed owner; companion/footer/capsule never duplicate it.
- [ ] Non-origin and unmount fallback behavior restores existing footer/capsule deterministically.
- [ ] Empty/loading/deleted/out-of-inventory integration passes without duplicate fetch/resource effects.
- [ ] No recordingApiClient, MarkdownEditor body, NodeView, or presentational component ownership violation.

## Gate commands
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-transcription typecheck
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-transcription test
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace typecheck
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace test

## Evidence
Record provider/subscription counts, exact registration API, origin/non-origin surface matrix, teardown fallback, forbidden-file diff check, and commands/results.

## Required completion record
- [ ] Implementation stays inside the stated ownership boundary.
- [ ] Exact gates pass with commands, changed files, and residual risks recorded.
- [ ] Focused cross-provider review passes; fix and repeat until clean before STATE impl:done.

Never record secrets, private transcript/audio content, tokens, Authorization headers, or internal artifact paths. Preserve unrelated changes and ownership boundaries.

## Exclusive capture-chrome file map
May edit the shelf integration modules and only the status/footer visibility seam in DocumentRecordingAction.tsx plus the narrow RecordingCapsule suppression consumer if required. Claim contextual ownership only through useWorkspaceRecordingFooterRegistration(filesystem, path, ownsSession). When the origin shelf owns it, DocumentRecordingAction keeps transcript/open/delete behavior but renders no competing status footer/elapsed/Stop; RecordingCapsule observes the same registry and stays hidden. Do not restructure TranscriptionRecordingProvider or edit inventory/playback/client/MarkdownEditor/NodeView ownership.
notes: Depends on the filesystem-owned above-editor chrome seam. The shelf consumes that seam and does not add orchestration to MarkdownEditor. It delegates Stop only when its exact document owns T08's contextual surface and suppresses the capsule through the single-owner registry; non-origin shelves show no capture controls. V1 adds no shelf Delete button; T10 remains canonical.

title: GH-579 T17e wire shelf-marker navigation, focus, and announcements
description:
## Goal
Connect T17 shelf rows and T16 markers bidirectionally, then complete keyboard, screen-reader, focus, announcement, and reduced-motion navigation behavior.

## Background
Ordinal/time is the primary association. Navigation must use the typed workspace-isolated T16 registry, never global DOM queries. T17d1 already owns all responsive layout and touch-target behavior.

## Scope
- Row Go to transcript invokes T16 focus/scroll command.
- Marker mouse/Enter/Space activation expands/focuses the owning shelf row.
- Apply brief non-content-mutating highlight at both destinations.
- Announce destinations and formatted seek value/time to assistive technology.
- Escape collapses and restores disclosure focus.
- Use prefers-reduced-motion only to choose instant versus animated focus/scroll movement; do not change layout or CSS.
- Resolve duplicate document panes deterministically through T16 identity.

## Non-goals
No API/playback state changes, global querySelector navigation, word-level sync, persisted preference, or visual redesign beyond approved spike direction.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Mouse and keyboard row-marker navigation work in both directions.
- [ ] Same paths in different workspaces/panes never cross-focus.
- [ ] Focus restoration, announcements, value text, and reduced motion pass.
- [ ] Highlight never mutates Tiptap document content.

## Gate commands
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-transcription typecheck
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-transcription test
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace typecheck
- pnpm --filter @hachej/boring-workspace test
- pnpm --filter workspace-playground typecheck
Pass means all commands exit 0 plus focused navigation/accessibility Playwright checks.

## Evidence
Record focus/announcement trace, workspace/pane isolation proof, focus restoration, highlight byte-stability, and reduced-motion movement result.

## Required completion record
- [ ] Implementation complete only in the stated ownership boundary.
- [ ] Exact gates below pass, or a concrete skip reason and replacement evidence is recorded.
- [ ] Evidence comment contains commands, relevant output, pass/fail, changed files, residual risks, and assignment token.
- [ ] Architecture/docs updated for public seams, otherwise explicitly record N/A.
- [ ] Cross-provider review passes; fix and repeat until clean before posting STATE impl:done.

Never record private transcript/audio content, tokens, Authorization headers, or internal artifact paths. Preserve unrelated changes, stable errors, workspace/editor isolation, reduced-motion/accessibility requirements, and guarded giant-file limits.

## Navigation/a11y ownership
This bead exclusively wires row-to-marker and marker-to-row focus/scroll, announcements, focus restoration, highlight, and reduced-motion movement on top of T17d's already-tested structural shelf accessibility. Do not rebuild shelf layout, disclosure, playback controls, or coarse-pointer sizing.

title: GH-579 T17f1 prove desktop real-media shelf behavior
description:
## Goal
Freeze deterministic real-backend desktop proof for multi-chunk decode/seek, shelf/marker/navigation, capture priority, deletion, and newest-50 behavior.

## Scope
- Create e2e/markdown-recording-shelf.spec.ts and deterministic non-private store/media fixtures.
- Seed two real playable multi-chunk recordings at different cursor positions and 51 synthetic manifests for truncation/out-of-inventory.
- Prove desktop Chromium decode, transition, seek, collapse, R#/time, marker navigation, contextual Stop/capsule exclusion, long+short mic pause-before-acquire, delete-audio preservation, and object-URL/request bounds.
- Freeze exact candidate identity and sanitized desktop artifacts for T17f2.
- Do not run mobile/responsive, final privacy sweep, aggregate package gates, or final reviews.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Named Playwright spec passes against the real backend with real decodable media.
- [ ] Desktop behavior, deletion, arbitration, truncation, and resource counters pass.
- [ ] Fixture/artifacts are deterministic, non-private, and reusable by T17f2.

## Gate commands
- pnpm --filter workspace-playground typecheck
- pnpm --filter workspace-playground build
- pnpm --filter workspace-playground exec playwright test e2e/markdown-recording-shelf.spec.ts --project=chromium-desktop

## Evidence
Record candidate/config, sanitized traces/screenshots, timings/counters, fixture hashes, commands/results, and residual desktop risks.

## Required completion record
- [ ] Implementation stays inside the stated ownership boundary.
- [ ] Exact gates pass with commands, changed files, and residual risks recorded.
- [ ] Focused cross-provider review passes; fix and repeat until clean before STATE impl:done.

Never record secrets, private transcript/audio content, tokens, Authorization headers, or internal artifact paths. Preserve unrelated changes and package/workspace isolation.

title: GH-579 relocate transcription storage under workspace assets
description:
## Goal
Relocate the workspace transcription control tree from root `.transcription/` into the existing workspace `assets/` area used by composer image uploads.

## Required path contract
- Canonical transcription control root becomes `assets/.transcription/` (alongside the existing `assets/images/` ecosystem while keeping the hidden transcription namespace).
- Active lease, recording manifests, recovery scans, inventory, deletion metadata, and all API/server handlers resolve exclusively through one shared path module beneath that root.
- Document-adjacent `.recording/<recordingId>/audio` and `/transcript` sidecars remain unchanged; this bead moves `.transcription`, not the frozen sidecar protocol.
- No root-level `.transcription/` writes remain.

## Scope
- Update shared constants/path helpers and every server consumer, test fixture, docs/proof, and ignore/visibility rule that currently assumes root `.transcription/`.
- Preserve workspace path validation, recording ownership, immutable manifest protocol, bounded sidecar reads, privacy, and no-private-path browser contracts.
- Define deterministic compatibility for workspaces containing the old root path: either an explicit bounded migration or an explicit unsupported/ignored policy with stable diagnostics; never silently merge two stores.
- Prove composer image upload behavior under `assets/images/` remains unchanged and transcription control files cannot collide with image names or upload cleanup.

## Non-goals
No relocation of document-adjacent `.recording` audio/transcript sidecars; no change to recording UX, audio format, transcription semantics, retention policy, or composer image upload directory.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Fresh recordings create no root `.transcription/`; lease/manifests are under `assets/.transcription/`.
- [ ] Recovery, inventory, chunk read/delete, transcription/materialization, and lease arbitration pass with manifests at the new root and sidecars unchanged.
- [ ] Old-root behavior is explicit, deterministic, tested, and documented.
- [ ] Existing `assets/images/` upload/read/Markdown behavior remains green.
- [ ] Source/import audit finds no production root-path literals outside the one canonical compatibility seam.
- [ ] Exact affected package typecheck/tests, invariants, import audit, and focused path/migration tests pass.

## Completion
Terra sole-writer implementation; fresh Sol review/fix loop until GREEN. Preserve unrelated dirt, never push, and record commits/gates/residual risks.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Scope clarification: move only the root .transcription control/manifests tree to assets/.transcription. Preserve the frozen document-adjacent .recording audio/transcript sidecar layout.

description:

## Scope

## Explicit exclusions

title: A1-dev local-development journey recut: P8 exit gate
description:
## Goal and why
A1-dev local-development journey recut: after the D1 EU-host exit, prove the compiled-bundle local developer workflow remains correct and record the resulting P8 exit evidence. The landed A1 compiler (#624) and landed P8 timing slice (#664) are context only; this bead is the bounded post-D1 recut required by the authoritative #391 graph.

## Scope
One reviewable proof-focused PR only. Exercise a compiled agent bundle from the documented local-dev workflow through the D1-compatible composition boundary, then update the P8/golden-path evidence only where the recut proves a changed contract. Preserve workspace-first authority and Decision 21: no pure mode.

## Explicit exclusions
No compiler rewrite, new runtime topology, provisioning system, scheduler, control plane, generic local-dev framework, or P2/package-extraction work. Do not turn a timing target into a release gate.

## Acceptance and proof
The exact local-dev journey is reproducible from clean inputs; its resolved composition/digest is deterministic; it neither bypasses workspace authorization nor introduces a pure-mode path. Record the command, redacted output, and timing versus the provisional P8 target in the authoritative evidence/runbook. This is the tracked A1-dev -> P8 gate; no new production feature is introduced.

## Risks / owner blockers
Post-D1 only: D1-006's three-binding EU proof and owner-approved runtime profile must be complete first. A failed local-dev proof blocks the P8 exit evidence rather than widening D1.

title: D1-004d2 Pi first-effect admission consumers
description:
## Goal and why
D1-004d2 Pi first-effect admission consumers: wire every agent first-effect through the D1-004d1 admission ledger — Pi service decorator, createAgent.ts facade, and slash/reload hooks all route through the admission check before any first effect lands.

## Scope
One reviewable PR. ≤400 net lines. Preserve Decisions 21–24, workspace-first authority, and no pure mode. Consumes the D1-004d1 admission ledger core; does not modify the ledger schema or advisory-fence mechanics themselves.

## Explicit exclusions
No Drizzle migration/schema changes, no rollback-journal reads/writes, no WorkspaceBridge operation wiring (that is D1-004d3), no scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, control plane, or unrelated refactor.

## Acceptance and proof
Focused unit/integration tests prove first-effect without a committed admission row is impossible across the Pi service decorator, createAgent.ts facade, and slash/reload hook paths. Stable codes reused from D1-004d1 (D1_ADMISSION_IDENTITY_MISMATCH, D1_ADMISSION_RECORD_FAILED — do not invent variants). Run targeted typecheck/tests and attach redacted output.

## Acceptance Criteria
Focused proof verifies first-effect without a committed admission row is impossible across the Pi service decorator, createAgent.ts facade, and slash/reload hook paths. Stable codes reused from D1-004d1 (D1_ADMISSION_IDENTITY_MISMATCH, D1_ADMISSION_RECORD_FAILED — must match these names exactly — do not invent variants).

Split per sizing audit 2026-07-13; sibling of D1-004d1/D1-004d3 (pre-declared in PR-PLAN).
notes: 2026-07-13: implementation dispatched after D1-004d1/#734; Pi decorator, createAgent facade, slash and reload first-effect paths only; stable admission codes reused.

No active replacement/removal, caller-triggered activation, scheduler, registry/provisioner, control plane, or retained-session disruption.

## Acceptance and proof
Focused unit and integration/host proof covers preload, pointer publication, stable-process swap, ingress-last order, served acknowledgement, lazy admission, and recovery handoff.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance field: focused proof verifies only this bead's owned contract.

SIZE FLAG (audit 2026-07-13): at the 400-line ceiling — if implementation overflows, STOP and pre-declare a 2-child stacked split before pushing.
acceptance_criteria:
Proof verifies bounded preload, durable pointer publication, stable-core atomic swap, ingress-last ordering, served acknowledgement, lazy admission, and recovery handoff. For an EU shared-host production start it verifies the accepted RuntimeIsolationEvidenceV1 digest/profile facts are consumed and material runtime-profile drift rejects until D1-006a reruns; qualification-only operations cannot claim public production exit.

Owned stable codes: D1_COLLECTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED (must match these names exactly — do not invent variants).

title: D1-004e recoverable unused-binding rollback fence
description:
## Goal and why
D1-004e recoverable unused-binding rollback fence: own the append-only rollback journal, sorted advisory-lock protocol, durable prepare record, and tuple-based recovery rules for unused additions. It defines and tests an injectable activation/served-ack protocol but does not wire a stable core or publish a production collection pointer.

## Scope
One reviewable PR. Preserve Decisions 21–24, workspace-first authority, and no pure mode. D1-005c consumes this journal protocol to perform concrete preloaded-core swap, durable pointer publication, ingress-last start, and served acknowledgement.

## Explicit exclusions
No stable-core preload/swap implementation, production collection-pointer write, ingress wiring, reconciler daemon, delete/update of journal rows, or unrelated refactor.

## Acceptance and proof
Focused unit/integration tests prove append-only prepare/recovery tuple behavior, sorted lock ordering, and fail-closed unknown combinations through the injectable protocol.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance field: focused proof verifies only this bead's owned contract.
acceptance_criteria:
Tests prove append-only journal and fail-closed tuple recovery for unused additions; concrete stable-core activation and served acknowledgement remain exclusively D1-005c scope.

Owned stable codes: D1_ROLLBACK_JOURNAL_FAILED (must match these names exactly — do not invent variants).

title: D1-006a EU runtime-profile qualification evidence
description:
## Goal and why
D1-006a EU runtime-profile qualification evidence: Run real hostile two-agent isolation probes on one EU host and produce redacted content-addressed RuntimeIsolationEvidenceV1 with test-suite digest and drift invalidation.

## Scope
Implement one coherent, reviewable PR only; preserve Decision 21–24, workspace-first authority, and the plan's no-pure-mode rule.

## Explicit exclusions
No scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, control plane, generic policy framework, secret/raw-path output, or unrelated refactor. Do not silently widen this bead.

## Acceptance and proof
Add focused unit tests at the public seam and integration/e2e or host-proof coverage where this task crosses a runtime boundary. Run the package typecheck, targeted tests, and the exact plan acceptance gate; attach redacted command output/evidence to the PR.

## Risks / owner blockers
May reject the candidate; no P2 extraction, direct downgrade, or forced runsc approval.

## Acceptance Criteria
Focused unit coverage plus the named integration, e2e, or EU-host proof passes. Stable-code, redaction, authority, and no-widening assertions must hold before merge.
acceptance_criteria: On one exact EU profile, automated hostile two-agent probes prove denial of sibling filesystem traversal, /proc/PID enumeration, signals/ptrace, mount/device access, process escape, cross-workspace network reachability, and secret access, while asserting resource ceilings and teardown. The redacted RuntimeIsolationEvidenceV1 binds profile facts and testSuiteDigest; a changed runtime binary, privilege model, platform mode, or material host policy invalidates it and leaves D1-006 blocked without downgrade.

title: ID1/BL1 owner gate: assign durable workspace-budget ownership and cap
description:
## Goal and why
ID1/BL1 owner gate: assign durable workspace-budget ownership and cap. Choose whether ID1-008 or BL1 owns the durable workspace spend cap, set the owner-approved cap, and preserve the rule that a hard cap exists before public exposure.

## Scope
Human decision gate only. Preserve Decisions 21–24, workspace-first authority, and no pure mode.

## Explicit exclusions
No runtime code, billing system, scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, policy framework, secret/raw-path output, or silent substitution of an owner decision.

## Required decision evidence
Record one committed authoritative decision/review entry that names the selected owner (ID1-008 or BL1), numeric cap and units, enforcement seam, public-exposure boundary, approver, and date. The entry must state that ID1-008's hard cap remains required before public exposure regardless of BL1 schedule.

## Risks / owner blockers
Blocks public self-service exposure, not Hydra plumbing.
acceptance_criteria: No code test is expected. Close only when a committed decision record assigns the durable workspace spend-cap owner (ID1-008 or BL1), states the approved cap and rationale, links the authoritative path/URL, and names approver and date.

title: Conditional P5a D1-consumed secret/readiness seam
description:
## Goal and why
Conditional P5a D1-consumed secret/readiness follow-up: execute only when a D1 qualification/evidence record identifies a real consumed secret/ref-from-env/file or boot-time readiness gap. It must not wait for a successful D1-006 exit when that gap prevents the exit.

## Scope
One reviewable narrow follow-up, or an evidence-only zero-code closure. Preserve Decisions 21–24, workspace-first authority, and no pure mode.

## Explicit exclusions
No Vault/KMS, rotation machinery, provisioning API, sandbox-provider selection/abstraction, scheduler, queue, registry, or control plane.

## Acceptance and proof
Close with either (a) a dated qualification/evidence record showing existing seams suffice and therefore zero code is required, or (b) the smallest D1-consumed seam with stable error codes plus focused proof that the recorded gap is resolved. Include the evidence path/URL, approver, and date.
acceptance_criteria: Close only from a dated D1 qualification/evidence record: either an explicit zero-code finding that existing seams suffice, or focused proof that the smallest recorded secret/readiness gap is resolved with stable errors. Record evidence path/URL, approver, and date.
